category_title: Nonverbal students with ASD using iPads for communication and academic tasks
scenario_title: Premature screen advancing during language sequencing app
minimal_context: The student is using the Speech with Milo app on an iPad in the classroom to learn prepositions.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: It is time for the Speech with Milo app. Touch Milo the mouse.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Touches Milo the mouse on the iPad.)

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Advances the screen before the audio description is completed.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's go back. Touch Milo and watch what he does.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Touches Milo the mouse, but advances the screen before Milo demonstrates the preposition.)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: It is time for the Speech with Milo app. Touch Milo the mouse.

STUDENT: (Touches Milo the mouse on the iPad.)

TUTOR: Listen to the preposition.

STUDENT: (Advances the screen before the audio description is completed.)

TUTOR: Let's go back. Touch Milo and watch what he does.

STUDENT: (Touches Milo the mouse, but advances the screen before Milo demonstrates the preposition.)

task.content: touching Milo the mouse, listening to a preposition named, and watching Milo demonstrate the preposition

context.materials: iPad, Speech with Milo: Sequencing app

challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: participant advanced the screen before Milo demonstrated the preposition

behavior_patterns.condition: When a flashcard appears, the animal must be tapped, and a description of the animal is provided
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: participant advanced the screen before the description was completed
